lesson.menu_desc: Book a time slot and politely reschedule when plans change -- 27 slides
lesson.subtitle: Aula 19 -- Making & Changing an Appointment -- Booking a Time & Moving It
lesson.title_tag: Professor View -- Percival JR | Aula 19 -- Making & Changing an Appointment

listenings.text: Hi Percival, this is Mr. Santos from the supplier. I would like to make an appointment to review the contract with you. Are you free midweek? I can suggest a time slot on Wednesday morning, around ten o'clock. Would that be convenient for you? If Wednesday does not suit you, I am flexible. We can arrange another day. Please let me know what works, and I will confirm it. I really want to fit this into your schedule, so just tell me your best time. Thank you very much, and I look forward to our appointment. Have a good day.

Hi Percival, this is Karen. Small problem with our appointment. The board meeting now clashes with our call on Wednesday, so I am afraid we have to reschedule. Could you write back to the supplier and politely ask to move it? Do not cancel, just postpone. Suggest Thursday at three instead, and ask if that day is convenient for him. Keep it kind and short. Say we are sorry for the change, and that we want to find a time that suits everyone. If Thursday does not work, ask him to propose another slot. Thank you so much, and let me know once it is arranged.

extra_audio.text: First, the supplier asks Percival to make an appointment and suggests a slot on Wednesday morning. Then, a board meeting clashes with that time. After that, Percival writes back and politely asks to reschedule. Later, he suggests Thursday at three and asks if it is convenient. Finally, the supplier agrees, and the new appointment is arranged.
